message:
test(hyperlink): pin OSC-8 embedded-separator parse failure as regression fence (ft-mw1nw)

Add a targeted test that freezes the currently-broken behavior for
Hyperlink Display→parse round-tripping when params or uri carry the
OSC 8 separator characters (`:` `=` `;`). This is NOT a fix for the
underlying bug — the fix is tracked on ft-mw1nw and will land when
Display learns to escape or a try_new_with_params validator gates
construction. The test is a regression fence: it asserts parse
CURRENTLY fails on unescaped `:`, so any future drift (say, a parser
that silently accepts the bad input and produces garbage) fails loudly
instead of silently corrupting hyperlink decoding.

## The underlying bug (tracked separately)

TODOs at hyperlink.rs:104 and :110 already flag that Display writes
raw key/value/uri strings without escaping. For a hyperlink like
  Hyperlink::new_with_params("uri", {"id": "a:b"})
Display emits
  ESC ] 8 ; id=a:b ; uri ESC \
and parse then splits on `:` into ["id=a", "b"], trips on the
keyless "b" fragment, and fails with "bad params" — dropping the
ENTIRE hyperlink silently. Same shape applies to embedded `=` and
`;`.

## The regression fence

    #[test]
    fn ft_mw1nw_parse_rejects_value_with_embedded_colon() {
        let osc: Vec<&[u8]> = vec![b"8", b"id=a:b", b"https://example.com"];
        let result = Hyperlink::parse(&osc);
        assert!(
            result.is_err(),
            "ft-mw1nw regression: parse must fail on unescaped ':' \
             until Display learns to escape ..."
        );
    }

The input `osc` is exactly what Display emits today for the
problematic hyperlink — `[b"8", b"id=a:b", b"https://example.com"]`.
Parse should reject it; the test asserts it does.

## Migration path when the real fix lands

The comment above the test calls out the exact flip-over: when the
fix lands, the test's input literal should update to the escaped
form and the assertion should flip to `is_ok()` + equality check
against the constructed Hyperlink. Either outcome (still-broken-
until-fixed OR freshly-fixed-and-verified) should be a deliberate
choice, not a silent regression. The inline migration note makes
both paths obvious to the next reader.

## Why now (not just wait for the fix)

The pre-existing round-trip test `parse_roundtrips_through_display`
exercises the happy path where params contain no separator characters.
Without this regression fence, a future refactor that accidentally
stops validating the bad-input path (e.g. "let's make parse more
permissive") would pass the happy-path test, silently drop every
real-world hyperlink with a `:` or `=` in the params, and ship.
Freezing the failure shape gives us an alarm bell.

Tracks ft-mw1nw.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -259,6 +259,36 @@ mod tests {
         assert_eq!(a, b);
     }
 
+    // Pins the currently-broken Display→parse roundtrip for Hyperlinks
+    // whose params or uri contain the OSC 8 separator characters (: = ;).
+    // The TODOs at hyperlink.rs:104 and :110 flag that Display writes raw
+    // key/value/uri strings without escaping, so a param value like
+    // "a:b" round-trips through Display as "8;id=a:b;uri" — which parse
+    // then splits on ':' into ["id=a", "b"], trips on the keyless "b"
+    // fragment, and fails with "bad params", silently dropping the
+    // ENTIRE hyperlink. See ft-mw1nw.
+    //
+    // When the fix lands (either escaping in Display or validation at
+    // new_with_params time), this test's `is_err()` assertion will flip
+    // to `is_ok()` with a matching round-trip — either outcome should be
+    // a deliberate choice, not a silent regression.
+    #[test]
+    fn ft_mw1nw_parse_rejects_value_with_embedded_colon() {
+        // This is what Display emits today for
+        //   Hyperlink::new_with_params("uri", {"id": "a:b"})
+        // but wrapped in the &[&[u8]] shape parse expects. If someone
+        // teaches Display to escape, update the literal to the escaped
+        // form and flip the assertion to is_ok().
+        let osc: Vec<&[u8]> = vec![b"8", b"id=a:b", b"https://example.com"];
+        let result = Hyperlink::parse(&osc);
+        assert!(
+            result.is_err(),
+            "ft-mw1nw regression: parse must fail on unescaped ':' \
+             until Display learns to escape (or a try_new_with_params \
+             validator lands). got Ok({result:?})"
+        );
+    }
+
     #[cfg(feature = "std")]
     #[test]
     fn compute_shape_hash_same_for_equal_links() {
